# mockr — AI Agent Skill

> mockr is a fast, zero-dependency CLI tool for mocking, stubbing, and proxying HTTP and gRPC APIs.
> Users install it via `npm install -D @ridakaddir/mockr` or download a binary.
> When a user asks you to create a mock API, generate mock config, or set up API stubs, use this reference.

---

## TABLE OF CONTENTS

1. [Installation](#installation)
2. [CLI Reference](#cli-reference)
3. [Configuration Overview](#configuration-overview)
4. [Route Fields](#route-fields)
5. [Case Fields](#case-fields)
6. [Path Matching](#path-matching)
7. [Conditions](#conditions)
8. [Named Path Parameters](#named-path-parameters)
9. [Directory-Based Stubs (CRUD)](#directory-based-stubs)
10. [Dynamic File Paths](#dynamic-file-paths)
11. [Template Tokens](#template-tokens)
12. [Cross-Endpoint References](#cross-endpoint-references)
13. [Array Processing ($each / $template)](#array-processing)
14. [Object Spreading ($spread)](#object-spreading)
15. [Type Conversion ($as)](#type-conversion)
16. [Dynamic Refs in Defaults](#dynamic-refs-in-defaults)
17. [Response Transitions](#response-transitions)
18. [Record Mode](#record-mode)
19. [CORS](#cors)
20. [Hot Reload](#hot-reload)
21. [API Prefix](#api-prefix)
22. [gRPC Reference](#grpc-reference)
23. [OpenAPI Generation](#openapi-generation)
24. [Proto Generation](#proto-generation)
25. [Common Patterns](#common-patterns)
26. [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting)

---

## INSTALLATION

```sh
# npm (recommended)
npm install -D @ridakaddir/mockr

# Binary (macOS ARM)
curl -L https://github.com/ridakaddir/mockr/releases/latest/download/mockr_darwin_arm64.tar.gz | tar xz
sudo mv mockr /usr/local/bin/

# Binary (macOS Intel)
curl -L https://github.com/ridakaddir/mockr/releases/latest/download/mockr_darwin_amd64.tar.gz | tar xz
sudo mv mockr /usr/local/bin/

# Binary (Linux x86-64)
curl -L https://github.com/ridakaddir/mockr/releases/latest/download/mockr_linux_amd64.tar.gz | tar xz
sudo mv mockr /usr/local/bin/

# Go install (requires Go 1.25+)
go install github.com/ridakaddir/mockr@latest
```

Verify: `mockr --version`

---

## CLI REFERENCE

### Root command: `mockr`

```
mockr [flags]
```

| Flag | Short | Default | Description |
|------|-------|---------|-------------|
| `--target <url>` | `-t` | | Upstream HTTP API to proxy unmatched requests to |
| `--port <n>` | `-p` | `4000` | HTTP server port |
| `--config <path>` | `-c` | `mockr.toml` or `.` | Config file or directory |
| `--api-prefix <path>` | `-a` | | Strip prefix before matching (e.g. `/api`) |
| `--init` | | | Scaffold a `mockr.toml` template + `stubs/users.json` |
| `--record` | | | Record mode: proxy and save responses as stubs (requires `--target`) |
| `--grpc-proto <file>` | | | Path to `.proto` file (repeatable; enables gRPC server) |
| `--grpc-port <n>` | | `50051` | gRPC server port |
| `--grpc-target <addr>` | | | Upstream gRPC server for proxy mode |
| `--help` | `-h` | | |
| `--version` | `-v` | | |

### Subcommand: `mockr generate`

```
mockr generate [flags]
```

**OpenAPI flags:**

| Flag | Short | Default | Description |
|------|-------|---------|-------------|
| `--spec <file\|url>` | `-s` | | OpenAPI spec file path or URL (required unless `--proto`) |
| `--out <dir>` | `-o` | `mocks` | Output directory |
| `--format <fmt>` | `-f` | `toml` | Config format: `toml`, `yaml`, `json` |
| `--split` | | `true` | One config file per tag; `--split=false` for single file |

**Proto flags:**

| Flag | Short | Default | Description |
|------|-------|---------|-------------|
| `--proto <file>` | | | Path to `.proto` file (repeatable; enables proto mode) |
| `--import-path <dir>` | | | Extra proto import search paths (repeatable) |

`--proto` takes precedence over `--spec` when both are provided.

---

## CONFIGURATION OVERVIEW

mockr supports three config formats, auto-detected by file extension:

| Format | Extension | Example |
|--------|-----------|---------|
| TOML | `.toml` | `mockr.toml` |
| YAML | `.yaml`, `.yml` | `mockr.yaml` |
| JSON | `.json` | `mockr.json` |

### Single file mode
```sh
mockr --config mockr.toml
```

### Directory mode
```sh
mockr --config ./mocks
```
Loads ALL config files in the directory, merged alphabetically by filename. You can mix formats.

### Auto-detection (no `--config` flag)
1. If `mockr.toml` exists in current directory, uses it
2. Otherwise, loads all config files from current directory

### Config structure
Two top-level arrays:
- `routes` — HTTP routes (use `[[routes]]` in TOML)
- `grpc_routes` — gRPC routes (use `[[grpc_routes]]` in TOML)

---

## ROUTE FIELDS

### HTTP Route

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `method` | string | required | HTTP method: `GET`, `POST`, `PUT`, `PATCH`, `DELETE` |
| `match` | string | required | URL path pattern (see [Path Matching](#path-matching)) |
| `enabled` | bool | `true` | Set `false` to disable without removing |
| `fallback` | string | | Case name served when no condition matches. Omit to proxy. |
| `conditions` | array | `[]` | Condition rules (see [Conditions](#conditions)) |
| `cases` | map | `{}` | Named response cases (see [Case Fields](#case-fields)) |
| `transitions` | array | `[]` | Time-based case progression (see [Transitions](#response-transitions)) |

### TOML example

```toml
[[routes]]
method   = "GET"
match    = "/api/users"
enabled  = true
fallback = "success"

  [routes.cases.success]
  status = 200
  file   = "stubs/users/"

  [routes.cases.empty]
  status = 200
  json   = '[]'

  [routes.cases.error]
  status = 500
  json   = '{"message": "Internal Server Error"}'
```

### YAML example

```yaml
routes:
  - method: GET
    match: /api/users
    fallback: success
    cases:
      success:
        status: 200
        file: stubs/users/
      empty:
        status: 200
        json: '[]'
      error:
        status: 500
        json: '{"message": "Internal Server Error"}'
```

### JSON example

```json
{
  "routes": [
    {
      "method": "GET",
      "match": "/api/users",
      "fallback": "success",
      "cases": {
        "success": { "status": 200, "file": "stubs/users/" },
        "empty": { "status": 200, "json": "[]" },
        "error": { "status": 500, "json": "{\"message\": \"Internal Server Error\"}" }
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

---

## CASE FIELDS

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `status` | int | `200` | HTTP status code (or gRPC status code for gRPC routes) |
| `json` | string | | Inline JSON response body. Supports template tokens. |
| `file` | string | | Path to JSON stub file. Relative to config directory. Trailing `/` = directory aggregation. |
| `delay` | int | `0` | Response delay in seconds |
| `persist` | bool | `false` | Enable persistence (CRUD operations) |
| `merge` | string | | Persistence strategy: `append`, `update`, or `delete` |
| `key` | string | | Field name used as filename for persist operations |
| `array_key` | string | | Array field name in stub JSON (for legacy single-file persistence) |
| `defaults` | string | | Path to defaults JSON file, deep-merged under request body |

**Rules:**
- Use `json` OR `file`, not both
- `json` supports template tokens (`{{uuid}}`, `{{now}}`, `{{timestamp}}`, `{{ref:...}}`)
- `file` paths support dynamic placeholders (`{query.x}`, `{body.x}`, `{header.x}`, `{path.x}`)
- File path ending with `/` aggregates all `.json` files in directory into an array
- `persist = true` requires `merge` to be set
- `defaults` file is deep-merged as base, request body fields win on conflicts

---

## PATH MATCHING

Routes are matched top-to-bottom in config order. First match wins.

### Exact match
```toml
match = "/api/users"
```

### Wildcard (`*`)
Matches one or more path segments.
```toml
match = "/api/users/*"           # matches /api/users/123, /api/users/abc
match = "/api/*/profile"         # matches /api/123/profile, /api/abc/profile
match = "/api/*"                 # matches /api/users, /api/users/123, etc.
```

### Named parameters (`{name}`)
Matches exactly one path segment and captures the value.
```toml
match = "/api/users/{userId}"                    # captures userId
match = "/api/{version}/users/{userId}"          # captures version and userId
match = "/api/orgs/{orgId}/teams/{teamId}"       # multiple params
```

Named parameters can be mixed with wildcards:
```toml
match = "/api/*/users/{userId}"  # wildcard + named param
```

### Regex (`~pattern`)
Prefix with `~` for full regex matching.
```toml
match = "~^/api/users/[0-9]+$"  # numeric IDs only
match = "~^/api/(v1|v2)/.*"     # v1 or v2 prefix
```

### Priority
Routes are evaluated in config file order. When using directory config, files are merged alphabetically. Place more specific routes before general ones.

---

## CONDITIONS

Conditions route requests to different cases based on request attributes.

### Condition fields

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `source` | string | Where to look: `body`, `query`, `header`, `path` |
| `field` | string | Field name (dot-notation for nested body fields) |
| `op` | string | Operator: `eq`, `neq`, `contains`, `regex`, `exists`, `not_exists` |
| `value` | string | Expected value (not used with `exists`/`not_exists`) |
| `case` | string | Case name to activate when condition matches |

### Sources

| Source | Description | Example field |
|--------|-------------|---------------|
| `body` | JSON request body. Supports dot-notation: `user.address.city` | `payment.method` |
| `query` | URL query parameters | `status` (from `?status=active`) |
| `header` | HTTP request headers | `X-User-Role` |
| `path` | Named path parameters or wildcard values | `userId` |

### Operators

| Operator | Description | Example |
|----------|-------------|---------|
| `eq` | Exact string equality | `value = "admin"` |
| `neq` | Not equal | `value = "banned"` |
| `contains` | Substring match | `value = "error"` |
| `regex` | Regex match | `value = "^[0-9]+$"` |
| `exists` | Field is present (no `value` needed). For `query`/`header`, empty values count as absent. For `body`, empty strings count as present. | |
| `not_exists` | Field is absent (no `value` needed). Inverse of `exists`. | |

### Evaluation order
1. Conditions evaluated top-to-bottom
2. First matching condition wins
3. If no condition matches, `fallback` case is used
4. If no `fallback`, request is proxied to `--target` (or 404 if no target)

### TOML example

```toml
[[routes]]
method   = "POST"
match    = "/api/checkout"
fallback = "standard"

  [[routes.conditions]]
  source = "body"
  field  = "user_type"
  op     = "eq"
  value  = "vip"
  case   = "vip"

  [[routes.conditions]]
  source = "body"
  field  = "user_type"
  op     = "eq"
  value  = "banned"
  case   = "blocked"

  [[routes.conditions]]
  source = "query"
  field  = "version"
  op     = "eq"
  value  = "v2"
  case   = "v2_response"

  [[routes.conditions]]
  source = "header"
  field  = "X-User-Role"
  op     = "eq"
  value  = "admin"
  case   = "admin_view"

  [[routes.conditions]]
  source = "body"
  field  = "payment.method"
  op     = "eq"
  value  = "crypto"
  case   = "crypto_payment"

  [[routes.conditions]]
  source = "body"
  field  = "coupon"
  op     = "exists"
  case   = "with_discount"

  [routes.cases.vip]
  status = 200
  file   = "stubs/checkout-vip.json"

  [routes.cases.blocked]
  status = 403
  json   = '{"error": "Account suspended"}'

  [routes.cases.standard]
  status = 200
  file   = "stubs/checkout-standard.json"

  [routes.cases.v2_response]
  status = 200
  file   = "stubs/checkout-v2.json"

  [routes.cases.admin_view]
  status = 200
  file   = "stubs/checkout-admin.json"

  [routes.cases.crypto_payment]
  status = 200
  file   = "stubs/checkout-crypto.json"

  [routes.cases.with_discount]
  status = 200
  file   = "stubs/checkout-discount.json"
```

---

## NAMED PATH PARAMETERS

`{name}` syntax in route `match` captures URL segments as named parameters.

```toml
match = "/api/users/{userId}"
# GET /api/users/42 -> userId = "42"
```

### Uses
1. **Dynamic file resolution:** `file = "stubs/users/{path.userId}.json"`
2. **Persistence key:** Body field fallback from path params
3. **Conditions:** `source = "path"`, `field = "userId"`
4. **Cross-ref placeholders:** `{{ref:stubs/{path.userId}/data.json}}`

### Key resolution priority (for `merge = "append"`)
When determining the filename for new records:
1. Request body field (matching `key` name)
2. Defaults file field
3. Named path parameters
4. Wildcard value (`*`)
5. Query parameters
6. Auto-generated UUID

### Security
- Path traversal (`..`) is prevented
- Hidden files (`.`) are rejected
- Unsafe characters are sanitized from path values

---

## DIRECTORY-BASED STUBS

Each resource is stored as a separate JSON file in a directory. This enables full CRUD operations.

### Directory structure
```
stubs/
  users/
    alice.json    -> {"userId": "alice", "name": "Alice", "role": "admin"}
    bob.json      -> {"userId": "bob", "name": "Bob", "role": "user"}
```

### Full CRUD config

```toml
# LIST — aggregates all files in directory into an array
[[routes]]
method   = "GET"
match    = "/api/users"
fallback = "list"

  [routes.cases.list]
  status = 200
  file   = "stubs/users/"

# CREATE — appends a new file to directory
[[routes]]
method   = "POST"
match    = "/api/users"
fallback = "create"

  [routes.cases.create]
  status  = 201
  file    = "stubs/users/"
  persist = true
  merge   = "append"
  key     = "userId"
  defaults = "stubs/defaults/user.json"

# GET ONE — single file via dynamic path
[[routes]]
method   = "GET"
match    = "/api/users/{userId}"
fallback = "detail"

  [routes.cases.detail]
  status = 200
  file   = "stubs/users/{path.userId}.json"

# UPDATE — shallow merge into existing file
[[routes]]
method   = "PATCH"
match    = "/api/users/{userId}"
fallback = "update"

  [routes.cases.update]
  status  = 200
  file    = "stubs/users/{path.userId}.json"
  persist = true
  merge   = "update"

# DELETE — remove file
[[routes]]
method   = "DELETE"
match    = "/api/users/{userId}"
fallback = "remove"

  [routes.cases.remove]
  status  = 204
  file    = "stubs/users/{path.userId}.json"
  persist = true
  merge   = "delete"
```

### Defaults file
Enriches POST/PATCH with server-generated fields. Deep-merged as base layer (request body wins).

`stubs/defaults/user.json`:
```json
{
  "userId": "{{uuid}}",
  "role": "user",
  "active": true,
  "createdAt": "{{now}}"
}
```

When a POST arrives with `{"name": "Charlie"}`, the result is:
```json
{
  "userId": "a1b2c3d4-...",
  "role": "user",
  "active": true,
  "createdAt": "2026-03-26T12:00:00Z",
  "name": "Charlie"
}
```

### How `merge = "append"` works
1. Reads request body JSON
2. Applies defaults (deep-merge, body wins)
3. Resolves `key` field value for filename
4. Creates `stubs/users/{keyValue}.json`
5. Returns the created record as response

### How `merge = "update"` works
1. Reads existing file
2. Applies defaults if configured
3. Shallow-merges request body over existing data
4. Writes back to file
5. Returns the updated record

### How `merge = "delete"` works
1. Deletes the file at the resolved path
2. Returns empty response with configured status code
3. Returns 404 if file doesn't exist

---

## DYNAMIC FILE PATHS

Use `{source.field}` placeholders in `file` paths to resolve them from the request.

### Placeholder sources

| Placeholder | Source | Example |
|-------------|--------|---------|
| `{query.fieldName}` | URL query parameter | `{query.username}` |
| `{body.fieldName}` | JSON body field (dot-notation) | `{body.user.id}` |
| `{header.HeaderName}` | Request header | `{header.X-Tenant}` |
| `{path.paramName}` | Named path parameter | `{path.userId}` |

### Examples

```toml
# Resolve from query: GET /api/orders?username=alice -> stubs/orders/alice.json
[[routes]]
method   = "GET"
match    = "/api/orders"
fallback = "byUser"

  [routes.cases.byUser]
  status = 200
  file   = "stubs/orders/{query.username}.json"

# Resolve from path: GET /api/users/42 -> stubs/users/42.json
[[routes]]
method   = "GET"
match    = "/api/users/{userId}"
fallback = "detail"

  [routes.cases.detail]
  status = 200
  file   = "stubs/users/{path.userId}.json"

# Resolve from body: POST /api/profile with {"id": "abc"} -> stubs/profiles/abc.json
[[routes]]
method   = "POST"
match    = "/api/profile"
fallback = "load"

  [routes.cases.load]
  status = 200
  file   = "stubs/profiles/{body.id}.json"
```

### Fallback behavior
If the resolved file doesn't exist, mockr falls through to the next matching condition or proxies to upstream.

### Security
- `..` (directory traversal) is blocked
- Hidden files (starting with `.`) are rejected
- Only `[a-zA-Z0-9_\-.]` characters are allowed after sanitization

---

## TEMPLATE TOKENS

Template tokens are replaced at response time in inline JSON, stub files, and defaults files.

| Token | Output | Example |
|-------|--------|---------|
| `{{uuid}}` | UUID v4 | `"a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890"` |
| `{{now}}` | RFC 3339 UTC timestamp | `"2026-03-26T12:00:00Z"` |
| `{{timestamp}}` | Unix milliseconds | `"1711454400000"` |
| `{{ref:path}}` | Cross-endpoint reference | See [Cross-Endpoint References](#cross-endpoint-references) |

### Usage in inline JSON

```toml
[routes.cases.created]
status = 201
json   = '{"id": "{{uuid}}", "createdAt": "{{now}}", "ts": "{{timestamp}}"}'
```

### Usage in defaults files

`stubs/defaults/item.json`:
```json
{
  "id": "{{uuid}}",
  "createdAt": "{{now}}",
  "status": "active"
}
```

### Request data placeholders in defaults
Defaults files also support request data placeholders (resolved at persist time):

| Placeholder | Source |
|-------------|--------|
| `{.field}` | Request body field |
| `{path.param}` | Named path parameter |
| `{query.param}` | Query parameter |
| `{header.Name}` | Request header |

Example defaults file:
```json
{
  "id": "{{uuid}}",
  "createdBy": "{header.X-User-Id}",
  "region": "{.region}",
  "environment": "{path.env}"
}
```

---

## CROSS-ENDPOINT REFERENCES

Use `{{ref:path}}` to embed data from other stub files into a response.

### Syntax

| Pattern | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `"{{ref:stubs/users/}}"` | Load all files in directory as array |
| `"{{ref:stubs/users/alice.json}}"` | Load single file |
| `"{{ref:stubs/users/?filter=role:admin}}"` | Filter array by field value |
| `"{{ref:stubs/users/?template=templates/summary.json}}"` | Transform each item with template |
| `"{{ref:stubs/users/?filter=role:admin&template=templates/summary.json}}"` | Filter + transform |

### Important: The entire `"{{ref:...}}"` value (including quotes) is replaced with the resolved data.

### Directory reference
```json
{
  "users": "{{ref:stubs/users/}}"
}
```
Resolves to:
```json
{
  "users": [
    {"userId": "alice", "name": "Alice"},
    {"userId": "bob", "name": "Bob"}
  ]
}
```

### Single file reference
```json
{
  "admin": "{{ref:stubs/users/alice.json}}"
}
```
Resolves to:
```json
{
  "admin": {"userId": "alice", "name": "Alice"}
}
```

### Filtering
```json
{
  "admins": "{{ref:stubs/users/?filter=role:admin}}"
}
```
Filters the array to only items where `role` equals `"admin"`.

**Multiple filters** (AND logic):
```json
{
  "activeAdmins": "{{ref:stubs/users/?filter=role:admin&filter=active:true}}"
}
```

**Nested field filtering** (dot-notation):
```json
{
  "results": "{{ref:stubs/orders/?filter=payment.status:completed}}"
}
```

### Templates
Transform each item in the referenced collection using Go `text/template` syntax.

`stubs/templates/user-summary.json`:
```json
{
  "id": "{{.userId}}",
  "displayName": "{{.name}}"
}
```

```json
{
  "users": "{{ref:stubs/users/?template=templates/user-summary.json}}"
}
```

### Nested references
Refs inside referenced files are resolved recursively. Circular references are detected and return an error.

### Empty results
If a directory is empty or no items match a filter, the result is `[]`.

### Security
- `../` (path traversal) is rejected
- Absolute paths are rejected
- All resolved paths must stay within the config directory
- Sensitive headers (`Authorization`, `Cookie`, `Proxy-Authorization`) are stripped and cannot be used in `{header.*}` placeholders within refs and defaults

---

## ARRAY PROCESSING

`$each` / `$template` syntax iterates over a collection and reshapes each item.

### Syntax

```json
{
  "items": {
    "$each": "{{ref:stubs/models/}}",
    "$template": {
      "modelId": "{{.id}}",
      "displayName": "{{.modelName}}",
      "version": "{{.version}}"
    }
  }
}
```

### Rules
- `$each` must be a `{{ref:...}}` that resolves to an array
- `$template` is the shape applied to every item, using `{{.fieldName}}` to access item fields
- Both `$each` and `$template` are required together
- Missing fields resolve to empty string
- Empty array from `$each` produces `[]`
- Non-array from `$each` produces an error

### Nested refs in templates
You can use `{{ref:...}}` inside `$template` values, including dynamic values from the current item:

```json
{
  "items": {
    "$each": "{{ref:stubs/countries/}}",
    "$template": {
      "code": "{{.code}}",
      "name": "{{.name}}",
      "cities": "{{ref:stubs/cities/?filter=country:{{.code}}}}"
    }
  }
}
```

### Combinable with `?template=` parameter
```json
{
  "items": {
    "$each": "{{ref:stubs/models/?filter=status:active}}",
    "$template": {
      "id": "{{.id}}",
      "label": "{{.name}} v{{.version}}"
    }
  }
}
```

---

## OBJECT SPREADING

`$spread` merges referenced object properties into the containing object.

### Syntax

```json
{
  "name": "My Endpoint",
  "$spread": "{{ref:stubs/configs/base.json}}",
  "override": "this-wins"
}
```

If `base.json` is `{"region": "us-east-1", "tier": "standard", "override": "base"}`, the result is:
```json
{
  "name": "My Endpoint",
  "region": "us-east-1",
  "tier": "standard",
  "override": "this-wins"
}
```

### Rules
- `$spread` value must resolve to a JSON object (not an array)
- Explicit properties in the containing object override spread properties
- The `$spread` key itself is removed from the output
- Works with dynamic placeholders: `"$spread": "{{ref:stubs/configs/{path.env}.json}}"`
- Nested spreading is supported

---

## TYPE CONVERSION

`$as` converts data from one type to another. Currently supports converting an array of objects into a single merged object.

### Syntax

```json
{
  "$as": "object",
  "from": "{{ref:stubs/deployments/endpoint-1/?template=stubs/templates/traffic-split.json}}"
}
```

### Supported conversions

| Target | Input | Output | JS equivalent |
|--------|-------|--------|---------------|
| `"object"` | `[{"a": 60}, {"b": 40}]` | `{"a": 60, "b": 40}` | `Object.assign({}, ...arr)` |

### Rules
- `$as` value must be a string (the target type)
- `"from"` field is required and must be a `{{ref:...}}` token
- For `"object"` conversion, source must resolve to an array of objects
- Later keys overwrite earlier ones on conflict
- Empty objects in the array are skipped gracefully
- Empty array produces empty object `{}`
- Unsupported target types produce a clear error
- Processing order: `$each/$template` -> `$spread` -> **`$as`** -> dynamic placeholders -> regular refs

### Example: Merge traffic splits from multiple deployments

```json
{
  "$spread": "{{ref:stubs/endpoints/{path.endpointId}.json}}",
  "trafficSplit": {
    "$as": "object",
    "from": "{{ref:stubs/deployments/{path.endpointId}/?template=stubs/templates/traffic-split.json}}"
  },
  "deployedModels": "{{ref:stubs/deployments/{path.endpointId}/?template=stubs/templates/deployed-model.json}}"
}
```

Where `stubs/templates/traffic-split.json` extracts the trafficSplit from each deployment:
```
{{json .deploymentSpec.trafficSplit}}
```

This resolves each deployment's `trafficSplit` into an array like `[{"dep-a": 60}, {"dep-b": 40}]`, then `$as: "object"` merges them into `{"dep-a": 60, "dep-b": 40}`.

---

## DYNAMIC REFS IN DEFAULTS

Defaults files support dynamic placeholders inside `{{ref:...}}` tokens. This enables environment-specific, tenant-aware, and request-contextual defaults.

### Placeholder types

| Placeholder | Source | Example |
|-------------|--------|---------|
| `{.field}` | Request body | `{.region}` |
| `{path.param}` | Named path parameter | `{path.tenantId}` |
| `{query.param}` | Query parameter | `{query.env}` |
| `{header.Name}` | Request header | `{header.X-User-Id}` |

### Example defaults file

`stubs/defaults/endpoint.json`:
```json
{
  "endpointId": "{{uuid}}",
  "tenantId": "{path.tenantId}",
  "environment": "{path.env}",
  "region": "{.region}",
  "createdBy": "{header.X-User-Id}",
  "models": "{{ref:stubs/environments/{path.env}/models/}}",
  "config": "{{ref:stubs/tenants/{path.tenantId}/configs/{path.env}.json}}"
}
```

### Behavior
- **Directory refs** (ending with `/`) are preserved as live tokens in the persisted file, so they resolve dynamically on each read
- **File refs** (specific files) are resolved at creation time and their concrete values are stored
- Template tokens (`{{uuid}}`, `{{now}}`) are resolved at creation time
- Request data placeholders (`{.field}`, `{path.x}`) are resolved at creation time

---

## RESPONSE TRANSITIONS

Time-based response progression. Two modes: request-time and background.

### Request-time transitions (GET routes)
Serve different cases based on elapsed time since first request. No file mutation.

```toml
[[routes]]
method   = "GET"
match    = "/api/orders/*"

  [[routes.transitions]]
  case     = "processing"
  duration = 30          # serve "processing" for the first 30 seconds

  [[routes.transitions]]
  case     = "shipped"
  duration = 60          # then "shipped" for 60 seconds (cumulative: 30-90s)

  [[routes.transitions]]
  case     = "delivered"
  # no duration = terminal state (forever after 90s)

  [routes.cases.processing]
  status = 200
  file   = "stubs/order-processing.json"

  [routes.cases.shipped]
  status = 200
  file   = "stubs/order-shipped.json"

  [routes.cases.delivered]
  status = 200
  file   = "stubs/order-delivered.json"
```

**Behavior:**
- Timer starts on first request to this route pattern
- `transitions[0]` is served immediately on first request (for its `duration` seconds)
- Durations are cumulative: transitions[0] 0-30s, transitions[1] 30-90s, transitions[2] 90s+ (terminal)
- The last entry (with no `duration` or `duration = 0`) is the terminal state served forever
- All requests to the same route pattern share the same timeline
- Conditions always take priority over transitions
- `fallback` is only used when no transitions are defined

**Important:** The `fallback` field is NOT served when transitions are defined. Place your initial state as `transitions[0]` instead.

### Background transitions (POST routes)
After a POST creates a resource, background goroutines mutate the file on disk after specified delays.

```toml
# POST creates the resource
[[routes]]
method   = "POST"
match    = "/api/deployments"
fallback = "create"

  [[routes.transitions]]
  case     = "ready"
  duration = 15          # 15 seconds after POST, mutate file

  [routes.cases.create]
  status   = 201
  file     = "stubs/deployments/"
  persist  = true
  merge    = "append"
  key      = "deploymentId"
  defaults = "stubs/defaults/deployment.json"

  [routes.cases.ready]
  persist  = true
  merge    = "update"
  defaults = "stubs/defaults/deployment-ready.json"
  # After 15 seconds, this case's defaults are merged into the created file on disk
```

`stubs/defaults/deployment.json`:
```json
{"status": "Deploying", "createdAt": "{{now}}"}
```

`stubs/defaults/deployment-ready.json`:
```json
{"status": "Ready"}
```

**Behavior:**
- Each POST spawns independent background goroutines — file mutations for different resources happen independently on disk
- Background goroutines sleep for cumulative durations, then apply the transition case's defaults via `persist.Update` (shallow merge into the file on disk)
- Transition cases for background mutations MUST have `persist = true`, `merge = "update"`, and `defaults` set
- Multiple stages have cumulative durations
- Requires at least 2 transitions (initial state + one deferred state)
- **Shared timeline per route pattern** — the transition clock is shared across all resource IDs on the same route (e.g. `POST /api/model/A/config` and `POST /api/model/B/config` share one clock)
- **Fallback on missing file** — if the transition clock has advanced to a terminal `update` case but the target file doesn't exist (new resource), mockr automatically falls back to the `fallback` case (e.g. `merge = "append"`) and creates the file normally
- **DELETE resets transitions** — when a DELETE route with `merge = "delete"` removes a resource, the transition clock for that match pattern is reset so subsequent POSTs start fresh
- If the file is deleted before a background transition fires, the mutation is skipped (logged as a warning)

### Transition fields

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `case` | string | Case name to serve or apply |
| `duration` | int | Seconds this state lasts. Omit or `0` for terminal (final) state. |

### Notes
- Conditions always take priority over transitions
- Hot reload resets all timelines
- No looping — transitions are one-way progressions
- DELETE with `merge = "delete"` resets the transition clock for matching routes
- Creating a new resource after a transition expires works automatically (falls back to the `fallback` case if the terminal case targets a non-existent file)

---

## RECORD MODE

Proxy all requests to a real API and save responses as stubs for offline replay.

```sh
# Record
mockr --config ./mocks --target https://api.example.com --record

# Replay (no target, no record)
mockr --config ./mocks
```

**Behavior:**
- First request to a path: proxied to target, response saved as stub, route auto-generated in config
- Subsequent requests: served from saved stub
- Response latency is saved as `delay` in the generated config
- `--record` requires `--target`

---

## CORS

mockr automatically adds CORS headers to all responses:
- `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *`
- `Access-Control-Allow-Methods: GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, HEAD, OPTIONS`
- `Access-Control-Allow-Headers: Content-Type, Authorization, X-Requested-With, Accept, Origin`
- `Access-Control-Expose-Headers: Content-Length, Content-Type`

OPTIONS preflight requests are handled automatically. No configuration needed.

---

## HOT RELOAD

mockr watches config files with `fsnotify`. Any change (edit, add, remove) to config files triggers instant reload.

**What reloads:**
- Route definitions
- Condition rules
- Case configurations
- Transition timelines (reset on reload)

**What doesn't need reload:**
- Stub files — read fresh on every request

### Cross-reference dependency tracking
When a persist operation (create/update/delete) modifies a stub file, mockr automatically detects if other routes reference that directory via `{{ref:...}}` and ensures subsequent reads return updated data. Changes are batched with 100ms debouncing.

---

## API PREFIX

Strip a prefix from incoming request paths before matching routes and forwarding to upstream.

```sh
mockr --config ./mocks --api-prefix /api
```

With `--api-prefix /api`:
- Incoming request: `GET /api/users`
- Matched against routes as: `GET /users`
- Proxied to upstream as: `GET /users`

Route definitions use the stripped path:
```toml
[[routes]]
method = "GET"
match  = "/users"    # not /api/users
```

---

## GRPC REFERENCE

mockr mocks unary gRPC methods from `.proto` files. No `protoc` or code generation required.

### Starting gRPC server
```sh
mockr --config ./mocks --grpc-proto service.proto
mockr --config ./mocks --grpc-proto users.proto --grpc-proto orders.proto
mockr --config ./mocks --grpc-proto service.proto --grpc-target localhost:9090
```

### gRPC route config

```toml
[[grpc_routes]]
match    = "/mypackage.UserService/GetUser"
fallback = "ok"

  [[grpc_routes.conditions]]
  source = "body"
  field  = "userId"
  op     = "eq"
  value  = "not-found"
  case   = "not_found"

  [grpc_routes.cases.ok]
  status = 0
  file   = "stubs/grpc/get-user-ok.json"

  [grpc_routes.cases.not_found]
  status = 5
  json   = '{"message": "User not found"}'
```

### gRPC route fields
Same as HTTP routes: `match`, `enabled`, `fallback`, `conditions`, `cases`, `transitions`.

`match` is the full gRPC method path: `/package.Service/Method`

### gRPC status codes

| Code | Name | Use for |
|------|------|---------|
| `0` | OK | Successful response |
| `1` | CANCELLED | |
| `2` | UNKNOWN | |
| `3` | INVALID_ARGUMENT | Bad request |
| `4` | DEADLINE_EXCEEDED | Timeout |
| `5` | NOT_FOUND | Resource not found |
| `6` | ALREADY_EXISTS | Duplicate resource |
| `7` | PERMISSION_DENIED | Auth failure |
| `9` | FAILED_PRECONDITION | |
| `13` | INTERNAL | Server error |
| `14` | UNAVAILABLE | Service down |
| `16` | UNAUTHENTICATED | No credentials |

### gRPC stubs
Stubs use protojson format (camelCase field names). Both snake_case and camelCase are accepted in conditions.

`stubs/grpc/get-user-ok.json`:
```json
{
  "userId": "user-123",
  "name": "Alice",
  "email": "alice@example.com",
  "createdAt": "{{now}}"
}
```

### gRPC conditions
Only `source = "body"` is supported for gRPC (not `query` or `header`). Dot-notation works for nested fields. All operators (`eq`, `neq`, `contains`, `regex`, `exists`, `not_exists`) work.

### gRPC proxy fallthrough
If a route has no `fallback` and no matching condition, the request is forwarded to `--grpc-target`. If no target is set, returns `UNIMPLEMENTED`.

### gRPC persistence
Same directory-based CRUD as HTTP. Full example:

```toml
# Create
[[grpc_routes]]
match    = "/items.ItemService/CreateItem"
fallback = "create"

  [grpc_routes.cases.create]
  status   = 0
  file     = "stubs/items/"
  persist  = true
  merge    = "append"
  key      = "itemId"
  defaults = "stubs/defaults/item.json"

# List
[[grpc_routes]]
match    = "/items.ItemService/ListItems"
fallback = "list"

  [grpc_routes.cases.list]
  status = 0
  file   = "stubs/items/"

# Get by ID (from request body field)
[[grpc_routes]]
match    = "/items.ItemService/GetItem"
fallback = "get"

  [grpc_routes.cases.get]
  status = 0
  file   = "stubs/items/{body.itemId}.json"

# Update
[[grpc_routes]]
match    = "/items.ItemService/UpdateItem"
fallback = "update"

  [grpc_routes.cases.update]
  status  = 0
  file    = "stubs/items/{body.itemId}.json"
  persist = true
  merge   = "update"

# Delete
[[grpc_routes]]
match    = "/items.ItemService/DeleteItem"
fallback = "delete"

  [grpc_routes.cases.delete]
  status  = 0
  file    = "stubs/items/{body.itemId}.json"
  persist = true
  merge   = "delete"
```

### gRPC transitions
Identical to HTTP transitions. Both request-time and background modes work.

---

## OPENAPI GENERATION

Generate a complete mockr config from any OpenAPI 3 spec.

```sh
# From local file
mockr generate --spec openapi.yaml --out ./mocks

# From URL
mockr generate --spec https://petstore3.swagger.io/api/v3/openapi.json --out ./mocks

# YAML format, single file
mockr generate --spec openapi.yaml --format yaml --split=false

# Then serve
mockr --config ./mocks
```

### What gets generated
- One config file per API tag (with `--split`, default) or one combined file
- One stub JSON file per response status code
- Path parameters converted to wildcards (`/pets/{petId}` → `/pets/*`)
- First 2xx response set as `fallback`

### Stub quality priority
1. Spec `examples` field — used verbatim
2. Schema `example` / `default` / `enum` — used directly
3. Schema synthesis — generates sample data from types and formats

### Format-aware synthesis

| Schema format | Synthesized value |
|---------------|-------------------|
| `uuid` | `"{{uuid}}"` |
| `date-time` | `"{{now}}"` |
| `date` | `"2026-01-01"` |
| `email` | `"user@example.com"` |
| `uri` / `url` | `"https://example.com"` |
| Field named `id` | `"{{uuid}}"` |
| Field named `name` | `"Example Name"` |
| `boolean` | `true` |
| `integer` | `1` |
| `number` | `1.0` |

---

## PROTO GENERATION

Generate gRPC mock config from `.proto` files.

```sh
# Single proto
mockr generate --proto service.proto --out ./mocks

# Multiple protos
mockr generate --proto users.proto --proto orders.proto --out ./mocks

# With import paths
mockr generate --proto service.proto --import-path ./vendor/protos --out ./mocks

# YAML format
mockr generate --proto service.proto --format yaml

# Then serve
mockr --config ./mocks --grpc-proto service.proto
```

### What gets generated
- Config file with `[[grpc_routes]]` for each RPC method
- Stub JSON files in protojson format per method
- Response fields synthesized from proto message definitions

### Field synthesis rules
Same format-awareness as OpenAPI: `id` → `{{uuid}}`, `email` → `user@example.com`, timestamp fields → `{{now}}`, etc.

---

## COMMON PATTERNS

### 1. Scaffold and start (quickest start)

```sh
mockr --init
mockr
```

### 2. Generate from OpenAPI and serve

```sh
mockr generate --spec openapi.yaml --out ./mocks
mockr --config ./mocks
```

### 3. Selective mock with proxy fallthrough

```sh
mockr --config ./mocks --target https://api.example.com
```

Only routes defined in config are mocked. Everything else proxies to the real API.

### 4. Full REST CRUD for a resource

Config file `mocks/products.toml`:
```toml
# List all
[[routes]]
method   = "GET"
match    = "/api/products"
fallback = "list"

  [routes.cases.list]
  status = 200
  file   = "stubs/products/"

# Create
[[routes]]
method   = "POST"
match    = "/api/products"
fallback = "create"

  [routes.cases.create]
  status   = 201
  file     = "stubs/products/"
  persist  = true
  merge    = "append"
  key      = "productId"
  defaults = "stubs/defaults/product.json"

# Get one
[[routes]]
method   = "GET"
match    = "/api/products/{productId}"
fallback = "detail"

  [routes.cases.detail]
  status = 200
  file   = "stubs/products/{path.productId}.json"

# Update
[[routes]]
method   = "PATCH"
match    = "/api/products/{productId}"
fallback = "update"

  [routes.cases.update]
  status  = 200
  file    = "stubs/products/{path.productId}.json"
  persist = true
  merge   = "update"

# Delete
[[routes]]
method   = "DELETE"
match    = "/api/products/{productId}"
fallback = "remove"

  [routes.cases.remove]
  status  = 204
  file    = "stubs/products/{path.productId}.json"
  persist = true
  merge   = "delete"
```

Defaults file `stubs/defaults/product.json`:
```json
{
  "productId": "{{uuid}}",
  "createdAt": "{{now}}",
  "inStock": true
}
```

### 5. Cross-endpoint references with filtering

```toml
[[routes]]
method   = "GET"
match    = "/api/dashboard"
fallback = "data"

  [routes.cases.data]
  status = 200
  file   = "stubs/dashboard.json"
```

`stubs/dashboard.json`:
```json
{
  "activeUsers": "{{ref:stubs/users/?filter=active:true}}",
  "recentOrders": "{{ref:stubs/orders/?filter=status:pending}}",
  "totalProducts": "{{ref:stubs/products/}}"
}
```

### 6. Multi-tenant with dynamic refs

```toml
[[routes]]
method   = "POST"
match    = "/api/{tenantId}/environments/{env}/endpoints"
fallback = "create"

  [routes.cases.create]
  status   = 201
  file     = "stubs/endpoints/"
  persist  = true
  merge    = "append"
  key      = "endpointId"
  defaults = "stubs/defaults/endpoint.json"
```

`stubs/defaults/endpoint.json`:
```json
{
  "endpointId": "{{uuid}}",
  "tenantId": "{path.tenantId}",
  "environment": "{path.env}",
  "region": "{.region}",
  "models": "{{ref:stubs/environments/{path.env}/models/}}",
  "config": "{{ref:stubs/tenants/{path.tenantId}/configs/{path.env}.json}}"
}
```

### 7. Record and replay

```sh
# Phase 1: Record real API
mockr --config ./mocks --target https://api.example.com --record

# Phase 2: Work offline
mockr --config ./mocks
```

### 8. HTTP + gRPC together

```sh
mockr --config ./mocks --grpc-proto service.proto --target https://api.example.com --grpc-target localhost:9090
```

### 9. Conditional responses for testing

```toml
[[routes]]
method   = "POST"
match    = "/api/payments"
fallback = "success"

  [[routes.conditions]]
  source = "body"
  field  = "amount"
  op     = "eq"
  value  = "0"
  case   = "invalid"

  [[routes.conditions]]
  source = "body"
  field  = "card.expired"
  op     = "eq"
  value  = "true"
  case   = "declined"

  [[routes.conditions]]
  source = "header"
  field  = "X-Test-Scenario"
  op     = "eq"
  value  = "timeout"
  case   = "timeout"

  [routes.cases.success]
  status = 200
  json   = '{"paymentId": "{{uuid}}", "status": "completed", "processedAt": "{{now}}"}'

  [routes.cases.invalid]
  status = 400
  json   = '{"error": "Amount must be greater than 0"}'

  [routes.cases.declined]
  status = 402
  json   = '{"error": "Card expired"}'

  [routes.cases.timeout]
  status = 504
  json   = '{"error": "Payment gateway timeout"}'
  delay  = 30
```

### 10. Array processing with $each/$template

```toml
[[routes]]
method   = "GET"
match    = "/api/catalog"
fallback = "data"

  [routes.cases.data]
  status = 200
  file   = "stubs/catalog.json"
```

`stubs/catalog.json`:
```json
{
  "products": {
    "$each": "{{ref:stubs/products/?filter=inStock:true}}",
    "$template": {
      "id": "{{.productId}}",
      "title": "{{.name}}",
      "price": "{{.price}}",
      "categories": "{{ref:stubs/categories/?filter=productId:{{.productId}}}}"
    }
  }
}
```

---

## TROUBLESHOOTING

### Common mistakes

| Problem | Cause | Fix |
|---------|-------|-----|
| Route never matches | Routes checked in order; a broader pattern above catches it first | Move specific routes before general ones |
| `file` returns 500 | Path is relative but config is in a subdirectory | File paths are relative to the config file's directory |
| `persist` does nothing | Missing `merge` field | Add `merge = "append"`, `"update"`, or `"delete"` |
| `merge = "append"` fails | `file` path doesn't end with `/` | Append requires a directory path: `file = "stubs/items/"` |
| `{{ref:...}}` returns `[]` | Directory is empty or filter matches nothing | Check path and filter field/value. Paths are relative to config directory. |
| gRPC route not matching | `match` not in `/package.Service/Method` format | Use full method path including package |
| `--record` fails | Missing `--target` | Record mode requires `--target <url>` |
| Template tokens not rendered in stub files | Using `{{uuid}}` in a file loaded via `file` | `{{uuid}}`, `{{now}}`, `{{timestamp}}` only work in inline `json` and `defaults` files. In stub files loaded via `file`, only `{{ref:...}}` tokens are resolved. Move dynamic tokens to inline `json` or a defaults file. |
| Dynamic file 404 | Placeholder value contains unsafe chars | Values are sanitized to `[a-zA-Z0-9_\-.]` |
| Conditions ignored | `source = "query"` or `"header"` in gRPC route | gRPC only supports `source = "body"` |
| Cross-ref security error | Using `../` or absolute paths in refs | Refs must stay within config directory; use relative paths |
| Defaults not merging | Expecting defaults to override body | Body wins over defaults (defaults are the base layer) |
| Fallback served with transitions | Using `fallback` alongside `transitions` | When transitions are defined, `transitions[0]` is served from the start — not the fallback. Put your initial state as the first transition entry. |
| Background transition not firing | Missing `persist`/`merge` on transition case | Background transition cases require `persist = true`, `merge = "update"`, and `defaults` to be set |

### Debugging tips
- Check terminal output: `via=stub` means served from mock, `via=proxy` means forwarded to upstream
- Use `enabled = false` to temporarily disable routes without removing them
- Use `delay` to simulate slow responses for timeout testing
- Test cross-refs by first curling the referenced path directly

---

## YAML CONFIGURATION REFERENCE

Complete YAML equivalent of all TOML examples for YAML-preferring projects.

### Full CRUD in YAML

```yaml
routes:
  # List
  - method: GET
    match: /api/users
    fallback: list
    cases:
      list:
        status: 200
        file: stubs/users/

  # Create
  - method: POST
    match: /api/users
    fallback: create
    cases:
      create:
        status: 201
        file: stubs/users/
        persist: true
        merge: append
        key: userId
        defaults: stubs/defaults/user.json

  # Get one
  - method: GET
    match: /api/users/{userId}
    fallback: detail
    cases:
      detail:
        status: 200
        file: "stubs/users/{path.userId}.json"

  # Update
  - method: PATCH
    match: /api/users/{userId}
    fallback: update
    cases:
      update:
        status: 200
        file: "stubs/users/{path.userId}.json"
        persist: true
        merge: update

  # Delete
  - method: DELETE
    match: /api/users/{userId}
    fallback: remove
    cases:
      remove:
        status: 204
        file: "stubs/users/{path.userId}.json"
        persist: true
        merge: delete
```

### Conditions in YAML

```yaml
routes:
  - method: POST
    match: /api/orders
    fallback: standard
    conditions:
      - source: body
        field: priority
        op: eq
        value: urgent
        case: urgent
      - source: header
        field: X-Api-Version
        op: eq
        value: "2"
        case: v2
    cases:
      standard:
        status: 200
        file: stubs/order-standard.json
      urgent:
        status: 200
        file: stubs/order-urgent.json
      v2:
        status: 200
        file: stubs/order-v2.json
```

### gRPC in YAML

```yaml
grpc_routes:
  - match: /mypackage.UserService/GetUser
    fallback: ok
    conditions:
      - source: body
        field: userId
        op: eq
        value: "not-found"
        case: not_found
    cases:
      ok:
        status: 0
        file: stubs/grpc/get-user.json
      not_found:
        status: 5
        json: '{"message": "User not found"}'
```

### Transitions in YAML

```yaml
routes:
  - method: GET
    match: /api/orders/*
    transitions:
      - case: processing
        duration: 30
      - case: shipped
        duration: 60
      - case: delivered
    cases:
      processing:
        status: 200
        file: stubs/order-processing.json
      shipped:
        status: 200
        file: stubs/order-shipped.json
      delivered:
        status: 200
        file: stubs/order-delivered.json
```

---

*This document is a complete reference for the mockr CLI tool. It ships with the `@ridakaddir/mockr` npm package at `node_modules/@ridakaddir/mockr/llms.txt`. For updates, see: https://github.com/ridakaddir/mockr*
